sanskrit:
नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः।
न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः॥
transliteration: nainaṁ chindanti śastrāṇi nainaṁ dahati pāvakaḥ | na cainaṁ kledayanty āpo na śoṣayati mārutaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Weapons do not cleave this (Self), fire does not burn it, waters do not wet it, and wind does not wither it.
contextual_meaning: Krishna introduces Arjuna to the changeless, invulnerable core of identity—the ātman—which transcends bodily harm, death, and all sensory threats.
psychological_state: Acute death-anxiety and ego-fragility; needs a re-frame from body-as-self to self-as-unchanging-awareness.
trigger_scenario: A soldier about to deploy, a patient awaiting risky surgery, or anyone facing irreversible loss who suddenly doubts their own continuity.
anchor_text: Your true self is unbreakable, untouched by fire, water, wind, or blade.
